sentences.en: Our social studies teacher gave us a big blank paper and told us to draw our whole family tree, going back as far as we could.
sentences.zh: 社會課老師給我們一張大白紙，要我們畫出整個家族的家譜，能追多遠就追多遠。

sentences.en: I started with myself and William, then drew lines up to Mom, Dad, and Sophia, our grandmother on Mom's side.
sentences.zh: 我從自己跟 William 開始，往上畫線連到媽媽、爸爸，還有 Sophia，也就是媽媽那邊的阿嬤。

sentences.en: On Dad's side, I drew my grandfather, who I have only met a few times because he lives in another city.
sentences.zh: 在爸爸這邊，我畫了我的爺爺，我只見過他幾次，因為他住在另一座城市。

sentences.en: Dad also has one older sister, my aunt, who has one daughter and one son, which makes them my cousins.
sentences.zh: 爸爸還有一個姊姊，我的姑姑，她有一個女兒和一個兒子，也就是我的表哥表姊。

sentences.en: My aunt's husband works far away for most of the year, so we usually only see him during the holidays.
sentences.zh: 我姑丈大部分時間都在外地工作，所以我們通常只在假期才會見到他。

sentences.en: Sophia told me that her own husband, my late grandfather on Mom's side, was born in a small village a very long time ago.
sentences.zh: Sophia 跟我說，她的先生，也就是我媽媽那邊已故的爺爺，很久以前出生在一個小村莊。

sentences.en: He died when I was still a baby, so I never really got to know him, but Sophia says I have his exact same laugh.
sentences.zh: 他在我還是嬰兒的時候就過世了，所以我其實從來沒真正認識過他，但 Sophia 說我笑起來跟他一模一樣。

sentences.en: Sophia said she was very young, only twenty years old, when they got married, which sounded incredibly young to me.
sentences.zh: Sophia 說她結婚的時候還很年輕，只有二十歲，聽在我耳裡覺得非常年輕。

sentences.en: William drew himself as a giant robot at the top of his family tree, which our teacher gently said was not exactly how a family tree works.
sentences.zh: William 在他家族的家譜最頂端把自己畫成一個巨大機器人，我們老師溫和地告訴他，家譜不是這樣畫的。

sentences.en: William argued that in his version of the family, he was simply born a little bit more advanced than the rest of us.
sentences.zh: William 爭辯說在他自己的版本裡，他只是比我們其他人天生更「先進」一點而已。

sentences.en: Bernice's family tree was much bigger than mine, since her mother comes from a family with five brothers and sisters altogether.
sentences.zh: Bernice 的家族樹比我的大很多，因為她媽媽那邊家裡總共有五個兄弟姊妹。

sentences.en: She said every holiday dinner at her house is basically a small festival, with more relatives than actual chairs.
sentences.zh: 她說她家每次過節聚餐基本上就像個小型節慶，親戚人數比椅子還多。

sentences.en: When I got home, I showed my finished family tree to Sophia, who pointed at my late grandfather's name and got a little quiet.
sentences.zh: 回家後，我把畫好的家族樹拿給 Sophia 看，她指著我已故爺爺的名字，安靜了一下。

sentences.en: She said drawing a family tree is a nice way to remember that everyone in it, even the people we never met, is part of why we exist today.
sentences.zh: 她說畫家族樹是個很好的方式，能提醒我們，樹上每一個人，就算是我們從沒見過的，都是我們今天會存在的原因之一。

sentences.en: I asked her if she missed my grandfather a lot, and she just said some kinds of missing someone never really go away, and that is okay.
sentences.zh: 我問她是不是很想念我爺爺，她只說有些思念永遠不會真正消失，而這樣也沒關係。

quiz.question: How old was Sophia when she got married?
How does William draw himself on his family tree?
options: As a giant robot at the top.

quiz.question: What does Sophia say about missing Coco's grandfather?
options: Some kinds of missing someone never really go away, and that is okay.
She never thinks about him anymore.
She refuses to talk about it.
